event: Madonna and Child with Saint Catherine and Saint Rosa
symbols: Virgin holding the Christ child — the Theotokos presenting the Word Incarnate
praying saint at left — Saint Catherine in contemplative devotion
saint at right — Saint Rosa gazing outward as witness to the faithful
Christ child blessing — the infant Savior dispensing grace to the saints and through them to the faithful
description: A half-length composition showing three women and an infant against a blue sky. At center, the Virgin Mary in a green mantle over a red dress holds the nude infant Jesus, who sits on her lap with one hand raised. At left, a young woman with golden hair and a blue garment looks downward with hands clasped in prayer, identified as Saint Catherine. At right, another young woman in a dark blue garment gazes outward, identified as Saint Rosa. The three women are arranged in a triangular composition with serene, idealized faces.
theological_description: This Sacra Conversazione brings together the Virgin and Child with two female saints in a timeless devotional encounter. The composition embodies the communion of saints: women from different centuries united in their relationship to Christ. The infant Jesus at center is the source and object of all devotion. Saint Catherine, traditionally identified with the learned virgin martyr of Alexandria, represents the intellectual pursuit of divine truth. Saint Rosa, whether of Lima or Viterbo, represents the flowering of holiness in the religious life. Mary's central position affirms her unique role as Theotokos — the God-bearer — through whom all access to Christ flows. The intimate scale and direct gazes create a devotional image inviting the viewer into the sacred company.

scripture_refs.reason: Blessed are you among women and blessed is the fruit of your womb — the Madonna and Child
Surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses — the saints gathered with the Virgin and Child
Fellow citizens with the saints — the communion depicted in the sacred conversation

_meta.title: Madonna and Child with St. Catherine and St. Rosa
